description: 'misa' asks why something happens or holds. An answer may give a reason, cause, motive, or purpose. 'lao' (BECAUSE) can frame a causal reply; 'lila' (PURP) marks a purpose.
articulatory_notes: Close and voice the lips for 'm'. Its resonance travels through the nose. Release into unrounded high-front 'i'. A slender midline groove just behind the top incisors produces 's'; finish on the open central vowel 'a'. Initial 'mi' carries the accent.
usage_notes: 'misa' normally appears in the reason position, directly ahead of the predicate. It announces the inquiry by itself. The clause takes no 'wa' (Q).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Questions about causes belong naturally to the examination of dependent origination. 'misa' asks for an explanation without deciding in advance that one cause must account for the whole event.
